title: eragrostis gangetica
intro: Eragrostis gangetica, also known as Bengal grass or finger grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The health benefits of Eragrostis gangetica include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce symptoms of arthritis, and act as an antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Eragrostis gangetica include infusions, decoctions, and powder forms, which can be used to create teas, capsules, and topical creams for various health issues.
